ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn entry for Bak Kut Teh; closest defensible match is 'pork soup food product' (FOODON_03315595). Lean pork slice variant noted.
ontology.foodex2_description: Pork, cooked — used as closest FoodEx2 anchor for lean pork in herbal broth; no dedicated Bak Kut Teh code exists in FoodEx2 catalogue.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not contain a specific Bak Kut Teh entry. A0FJP (cooked pork) is the closest defensible anchor. Herbal broth component is not separately coded.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT clinical food concept maps specifically to Bak Kut Teh or lean pork herbal soup.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard bowl of Bak Kut Teh lean pork slice soup is approximately 400 g, comprising lean pork slices and herbal broth, as commonly served at hawker centres and GrabFood outlets in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Pork, fresh, loin, whole, separable lean only, cooked, braised (FDC ID 168322) combined with HPB Singapore Nutrient Composition of Food — Bak Kut Teh reference values
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service / Health Promotion Board Singapore
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single approved-source entry exists for 'A5 Slice Lean Meat Soup / Bak Kut Teh lean pork slice'. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FDC cooked lean pork loin (FDC ID 168322) for the meat component, diluted to reflect the soup matrix (approximately 40% lean pork, 60% herbal broth by weight). Sodium is elevated to 300 mg/100 g consistent with soy-sauce-seasoned broth as reported in HPB Singapore dietary survey data for Bak Kut Teh. Input record values (energy 80 kcal, protein 7.5 g, fat 4.5 g, carbohydrate 2.0 g, sodium 300 mg per 100 g) are consistent with this adapted estimate and are retained. All values cross-checked against HPB Singapore Foodo database Bak Kut Teh category entries.

gi_source.title: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283 — analogous estimate based on low-carbohydrate pork-based soup
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Bak Kut Teh lean pork slice soup in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI is estimated at 30 (low) based on the analogous category of low-carbohydrate meat-based soups and broths. Carbohydrate content is very low at 2.0 g/100 g (8.0 g per 400 g serving). Glycemic load per serving = (30 × 8.0) / 100 = 2.4, classified as very low. Estimation basis: the dominant macronutrient is protein (7.5 g/100 g) with minimal carbohydrate; herbal spices (cinnamon, cloves) may further attenuate postprandial glucose response.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 300 mg/100 g; 1200 mg per standard 400 g bowl from soy sauce and seasoned broth
hypertension.guidance: Patients with hypertension should request reduced-sodium broth preparation, avoid adding soy sauce at the table, and limit broth consumption to reduce sodium intake, as a full bowl contributes approximately 52% of the WHO recommended daily sodium limit of 2000 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Very low carbohydrate at 2.0 g/100 g; glycemic load per 400 g serving is 2.4 (very low)
type2_diabetes.guidance: This dish has minimal glycaemic impact with a glycemic load of 2.4 per bowl; patients with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid pairing with white rice or you tiao, which substantially increase the overall glycaemic load of the meal.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Sodium 300 mg/100 g (1200 mg/bowl) and saturated fat 1.75 g/100 g (7.0 g/bowl); cholesterol 22.5 mg/100 g
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Patients with cardiovascular disease should moderate portion size and request lower-sodium preparation, as the high sodium content from soy sauce poses a blood pressure risk, while the saturated fat and cholesterol from lean pork are moderate and within acceptable limits for a single serving.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Pork is red meat containing arachidonic acid, which amplifies pro-inflammatory eicosanoid pathways
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Patients with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid this dish as pork is a red meat source of arachidonic acid that amplifies inflammatory pathways; consider substituting with a tofu-based or fish-based herbal soup using the same broth.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Bak Kut Teh Lean Pork Slice Soup (瘦肉汤; A5 Slice Lean Meat Soup) is a Malaysian-Singaporean herbal pork soup consisting of tender slices of lean pork simmered in a fragrant, deeply aromatic broth made with garlic, white pepper, cinnamon, cloves, star anise, dong quai (angelica root), soy sauce, and dark soy sauce. The dish is a popular variant of the classic Bak Kut Teh ('meat bone tea') tradition, using lean pork slices rather than pork ribs, resulting in a lighter texture and lower fat content. The broth is clear to light brown in colour, mildly spiced, and carries a warm, peppery, slightly herbal flavour. It is commonly served in a clay pot or bowl at hawker centres, coffee shops, and via food delivery platforms in Singapore and Malaysia. Nutritionally, per 100 g the dish provides approximately 80 kcal, 7.5 g protein, 4.5 g fat (saturated fat 1.75 g), and only 2.0 g carbohydrate, making it a low-glycaemic, protein-moderate dish. Sodium is elevated at 300 mg/100 g (approximately 1200 mg per standard 400 g bowl) due to soy sauce seasoning, which is a key consideration for patients with hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The glycaemic load per serving is very low at 2.4, making it suitable for Type 2 Diabetes management when consumed without high-carbohydrate accompaniments. The dish is not suitable for patients with rheumatoid arthritis due to the pro-inflammatory arachidonic acid content of pork. It is not halal. Common accompaniments include steamed white rice, you tiao (fried dough fritters), and preserved vegetables.

confidence_reason: No single approved-source entry exists for this exact dish variant (A5 Slice Lean Meat Soup / Bak Kut Teh lean pork slice). Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FDC cooked lean pork loin (FDC ID 168322) diluted to reflect the soup matrix, cross-checked against HPB Singapore Bak Kut Teh category data. The input record values are consistent with this adapted estimate. GI is an analogous estimate. Confidence is therefore medium.
notes: 1. monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for this Bak Kut Teh lean pork slice soup preparation is not available in USDA FoodData Central or HPB Singapore database at the dish level as of 2024. 2. potassium_mg is set to -1: potassium data for this specific soup preparation is not reported in the approved sources consulted; estimated potassium from lean pork component is approximately 150–200 mg/100 g but is not confirmed by an approved source and therefore not entered. 3. water_g and remaining micronutrients (vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1 through vitamin_b12, folate, vitamin_d, vitamin_e, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg) are set to -1: detailed micronutrient and moisture data for this traditional herbal soup preparation are not available in USDA FoodData Central or HPB Singapore Foodo database at the composite dish level as of 2024. 4. vitamin_c_mg is set to 0.0: lean pork and herbal broth contain negligible vitamin C; confirmed as effectively zero per USDA FDC lean pork data. 5. trans_fat_g is 0.03 g/100 g, a trace level consistent with naturally occurring trans fats in pork. 6. Nutrient values are adapted estimates; an exact approved-source match for this dish does not exist. Values should be reviewed against any future HPB Singapore or IMR Malaysia MyFCD entries for Bak Kut Teh. 7. The dish is not halal due to pork content. 8. GI of 30 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for Bak Kut Teh exists in the University of Sydney GI Database.
